```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=15, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
  df<-as.data.frame(cropBRA,rev=2)
  names(df)<-c("lon","lat","iso","year","crop","w","value")
  df <- df %>%
    group_by(lon, lat) %>%
    summarise(value = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  df$area_cell<-calArea(df$lon,df$lat,res=0.5,mha=1)
  df$newvalues<-df$area_cell*df$value*100 #ha
  Total_ha=sum(df$newvalues, na.rm = TRUE ) #unit: ha
  Total_Mha=Total_ha/1e6 #unit: Mha

  BRA<-subset(map, country=='BRA')
  coords <- strsplit(BRA$cell, "\\.")  
  mat    <- do.call(rbind, coords) 
  BRA$lon <- mat[,1]
  BRA$lat <- mat[,2]
  BRA$iso <- mat[,3]
  BRA$lon <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lon))
  BRA$lat <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lat))
  
  dfmap <- BRA %>%
    left_join(df, by = c("lon", "lat"))
  
  r <- rasterFromXYZ(cbind(df[,c("lon","lat",
                                   "value","newvalues")], z=1), 
                     res = c(min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lon)))),
                             min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lat))))))
  
  polys <- rasterToPolygons(r, dissolve = FALSE)
  
  crs(polys) <- CRS("+init=EPSG:4326")
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_as_sf(polys) %>%
      st_transform(st_crs(biomes))
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_make_valid(cells_sf)
    biomes <- st_make_valid(biomes)
  
  
  int_sf <- st_intersection(
      cells_sf  %>% mutate(cell_id = row_number()),
      biomes %>% dplyr::select(CD_LEGEN1))
  
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      filter(!st_is_empty(.)) %>%
      st_collection_extract("POLYGON")
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      mutate(area = st_area(geometry))
  
  majoritario <- int_sf %>%
      group_by(cell_id) %>%
      slice_max(area, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      dplyr::select(cell_id, CD_LEGEN1)
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_sf %>%
      mutate(cell_id = row_number()) %>%
      left_join(
        st_set_geometry(majoritario, NULL),
        by = "cell_id"
      ) %>%
      dplyr::select(-cell_id)
  
  cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1 <- 
      iconv(cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1, from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8")
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_bioma %>%
      mutate(CD_LEGEN1 = factor(CD_LEGEN1,
                              levels = sort(unique(CD_LEGEN1))))
  
  #vmax <- max(cells_bioma$value, na.rm = TRUE)
  vmax<-0.05
  mean_area<-0.5*vmax
  myplot<-ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data=cells_bioma, aes(fill = value), color=NA) +
    geom_sf(data = biomes, fill = NA, color = "gray", size = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low     = "white",        
      mid     = "orange",       
      high    = "darkred",        
      midpoint= mean_area,
      na.value= "grey",
      name     = "", 
      limits    = c(0, vmax),
      oob    = squish ,
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Rice",
      subtitle = y,
      caption  = "Source: MAgPIE Default",
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "white")
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank()
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_colorbar(
      barwidth      = unit(0.6, "cm"),
      barheight     = unit(2.5,   "cm"),
    )) +
    theme(
      plot.title        = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = 11),
      plot.caption      = element_text(size = 11),
      legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"),
      legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(size = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor  = element_line(size = 0.25)
    )
    plots<-append(plots, list(myplot))
}
wrap_plots(plots, nrow=1)
```

Following the same pattern observed for harvested area, MAgPIE estimates for rice production exceed the official data, as shown below.

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
prod_rice_pam <- c(11.24,12.30,11.09)
prod_rice_mag <- c(15.71,17.30,16.86)

erro <- (prod_rice_mag - prod_rice_pam)/prod_rice_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = prod_rice_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = prod_rice_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Rice production (Mt).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "Mt")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, Mt, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```

Table and figure below present the yield data, showing that the model outputs are higher than the observed values. However, the estimation errors are small, particularly in 2015 and 2020, at $+0.70\%$ and $+4.54\%$, 

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
yield_rice_pam <- c(4.13,5.75,6.61)
yield_rice_mag <- c(5.22,5.79,6.91)

erro <- (yield_rice_mag - yield_rice_pam)/yield_rice_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = yield_rice_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = yield_rice_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Rice yields (t/ha).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "t_per_ha")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, t_per_ha, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```

### Groundnut

The total harvested area of groundnut for the years 2010, 2015, and 2020 is presented below.   

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

ha_groundnut_pam <- c(0.09,0.15,0.18)
ha_groundnut_mag <- c(0.06,0.03,0.03)

erro <- (ha_groundnut_mag - ha_groundnut_pam)/ha_groundnut_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = ha_groundnut_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = ha_groundnut_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Groundnut harvested area (Mha).")
```

Although PAM / IBGE data indicate an expansion in the harvested area, MAgPIE estimates suggest a decline in this crop, with deviations reaching $-83.33\%$ in 2020.

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "Mha")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, Mha, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```


```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=15, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

plots <- list() 
for (y in years){
  cropBRA<-crop['BRA',y,'groundnut']
  df<-as.data.frame(cropBRA,rev=2)
  names(df)<-c("lon","lat","iso","year","crop","w","value")
  df <- df %>%
    group_by(lon, lat) %>%
    summarise(value = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  df$area_cell<-calArea(df$lon,df$lat,res=0.5,mha=1)
  df$newvalues<-df$area_cell*df$value*100 #ha
  Total_ha=sum(df$newvalues, na.rm = TRUE ) #unit: ha
  Total_Mha=Total_ha/1e6 #unit: Mha

  BRA<-subset(map, country=='BRA')
  coords <- strsplit(BRA$cell, "\\.")  
  mat    <- do.call(rbind, coords) 
  BRA$lon <- mat[,1]
  BRA$lat <- mat[,2]
  BRA$iso <- mat[,3]
  BRA$lon <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lon))
  BRA$lat <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lat))
  
  dfmap <- BRA %>%
    left_join(df, by = c("lon", "lat"))
  
  r <- rasterFromXYZ(cbind(df[,c("lon","lat",
                                   "value","newvalues")], z=1), 
                     res = c(min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lon)))),
                             min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lat))))))
  
  polys <- rasterToPolygons(r, dissolve = FALSE)
  
  crs(polys) <- CRS("+init=EPSG:4326")
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_as_sf(polys) %>%
      st_transform(st_crs(biomes))
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_make_valid(cells_sf)
    biomes <- st_make_valid(biomes)
  
  
  int_sf <- st_intersection(
      cells_sf  %>% mutate(cell_id = row_number()),
      biomes %>% dplyr::select(CD_LEGEN1))
  
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      filter(!st_is_empty(.)) %>%
      st_collection_extract("POLYGON")
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      mutate(area = st_area(geometry))
  
  majoritario <- int_sf %>%
      group_by(cell_id) %>%
      slice_max(area, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      dplyr::select(cell_id, CD_LEGEN1)
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_sf %>%
      mutate(cell_id = row_number()) %>%
      left_join(
        st_set_geometry(majoritario, NULL),
        by = "cell_id"
      ) %>%
      dplyr::select(-cell_id)
  
  cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1 <- 
      iconv(cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1, from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8")
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_bioma %>%
      mutate(CD_LEGEN1 = factor(CD_LEGEN1,
                              levels = sort(unique(CD_LEGEN1))))
  
  #vmax <- max(cells_bioma$value, na.rm = TRUE)
  vmax<-0.01
  mean_area<-0.5*vmax
  myplot<-ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data=cells_bioma, aes(fill = value), color=NA) +
    geom_sf(data = biomes, fill = NA, color = "gray", size = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low     = "white",        
      mid     = "orange",       
      high    = "darkred",        
      midpoint= mean_area,
      na.value= "grey",
      name     = "", 
      limits    = c(0, vmax),
      oob    = squish ,
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Groundnut",
      subtitle = y,
      caption  = "Source: MAgPIE Default",
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "white")
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank()
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_colorbar(
      barwidth      = unit(0.6, "cm"),
      barheight     = unit(2.5,   "cm"),
    )) +
    theme(
      plot.title        = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = 11),
      plot.caption      = element_text(size = 11),
      legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"),
      legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(size = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor  = element_line(size = 0.25)
    )
    plots<-append(plots, list(myplot))
}
wrap_plots(plots, nrow=1)
```

The data for groundnut production are presented below, indicating that the model underestimated values for three years analyzed. The smallest deviation occurred in 2015, $-34\%$,  while the largest was observed in 2020 ($-93.85\%$).

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
prod_groudn_pam <- c(0.26,0.50,0.65)
prod_groudn_mag <- c(0.07,0.33,0.04)

erro <- (prod_groudn_mag - prod_groudn_pam)/prod_groudn_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = prod_groudn_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = prod_groudn_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Groundnut production (Mt).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "Mt")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, Mt, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```

Table and figure below present the yield values, revealing a distinct pattern in which the model underestimates values in 2010 and 2020, but shows a positive deviation of $225\%$ in 2015. This large deviation corresponds to the year with the highest model-estimated production ($0.33$ Mt), despite a harvested area of only $0.03$ Mha.

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
yield_groundn_pam <- c(2.77,3.38,3.64)
yield_groundn_mag <- c(1.17,11.0,1.33)

erro <- (yield_groundn_mag - yield_groundn_pam)/yield_groundn_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = yield_groundn_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = yield_groundn_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Groundnut yields (t/ha).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "t_per_ha")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, t_per_ha, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```

### Sunflower

The total harvested area of sunflower for the years 2010, 2015, and 2020 is presented below.   

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

ha_sunflower_pam <- c(0.08,0.11,0.05)
ha_sunflower_mag <- c(0.01,0.02,0.02)

erro <- (ha_sunflower_mag - ha_sunflower_pam)/ha_sunflower_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = ha_sunflower_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = ha_sunflower_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Sunflower harvested area (Mha).")
```

The comparison between PAM/IBGE and MAgPIE outputs reveals that sunflower cultivation is strongly underestimated by the model. Across all reference years, the harvested area reported by MAgPIE is considerably smaller than official data, with errors ranging from $-60\%$ to $-88\%$. While PAM/IBGE records show moderate but fluctuating sunflower cultivation in southern Brazil, MAgPIE results suggest a very limited and spatially diffuse allocation, failing to capture the concentration of this crop in specific producing regions.

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "Mha")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, Mha, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```


```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=15, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

plots <- list() 
for (y in years){
  cropBRA<-crop['BRA',y,'sunflower']
  df<-as.data.frame(cropBRA,rev=2)
  names(df)<-c("lon","lat","iso","year","crop","w","value")
  df <- df %>%
    group_by(lon, lat) %>%
    summarise(value = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  df$area_cell<-calArea(df$lon,df$lat,res=0.5,mha=1)
  df$newvalues<-df$area_cell*df$value*100 #ha
  Total_ha=sum(df$newvalues, na.rm = TRUE ) #unit: ha
  Total_Mha=Total_ha/1e6 #unit: Mha

  BRA<-subset(map, country=='BRA')
  coords <- strsplit(BRA$cell, "\\.")  
  mat    <- do.call(rbind, coords) 
  BRA$lon <- mat[,1]
  BRA$lat <- mat[,2]
  BRA$iso <- mat[,3]
  BRA$lon <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lon))
  BRA$lat <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lat))
  
  dfmap <- BRA %>%
    left_join(df, by = c("lon", "lat"))
  
  r <- rasterFromXYZ(cbind(df[,c("lon","lat",
                                   "value","newvalues")], z=1), 
                     res = c(min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lon)))),
                             min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lat))))))
  
  polys <- rasterToPolygons(r, dissolve = FALSE)
  
  crs(polys) <- CRS("+init=EPSG:4326")
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_as_sf(polys) %>%
      st_transform(st_crs(biomes))
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_make_valid(cells_sf)
    biomes <- st_make_valid(biomes)
  
  
  int_sf <- st_intersection(
      cells_sf  %>% mutate(cell_id = row_number()),
      biomes %>% dplyr::select(CD_LEGEN1))
  
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      filter(!st_is_empty(.)) %>%
      st_collection_extract("POLYGON")
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      mutate(area = st_area(geometry))
  
  majoritario <- int_sf %>%
      group_by(cell_id) %>%
      slice_max(area, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      dplyr::select(cell_id, CD_LEGEN1)
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_sf %>%
      mutate(cell_id = row_number()) %>%
      left_join(
        st_set_geometry(majoritario, NULL),
        by = "cell_id"
      ) %>%
      dplyr::select(-cell_id)
  
  cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1 <- 
      iconv(cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1, from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8")
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_bioma %>%
      mutate(CD_LEGEN1 = factor(CD_LEGEN1,
                              levels = sort(unique(CD_LEGEN1))))
  
  #vmax <- max(cells_bioma$value, na.rm = TRUE)
  vmax<-0.006
  mean_area<-0.5*vmax
  myplot<-ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data=cells_bioma, aes(fill = value), color=NA) +
    geom_sf(data = biomes, fill = NA, color = "gray", size = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low     = "white",        
      mid     = "orange",       
      high    = "darkred",        
      midpoint= mean_area,
      na.value= "grey",
      name     = "", 
      limits    = c(0, vmax),
      oob    = squish ,
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Sunflower",
      subtitle = y,
      caption  = "Source: MAgPIE Default",
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "white")
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank()
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_colorbar(
      barwidth      = unit(0.6, "cm"),
      barheight     = unit(2.5,   "cm"),
    )) +
    theme(
      plot.title        = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = 11),
      plot.caption      = element_text(size = 11),
      legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"),
      legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(size = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor  = element_line(size = 0.25)
    )
    plots<-append(plots, list(myplot))
}
wrap_plots(plots, nrow=1)
```

Table and figure below present sunflower production values, showing that despite the smaller harvested area compared to official data, production in 2020 exceeded observed values by $50\%$.

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
prod_sunflower_pam <- c(0.09,0.16,0.08)
prod_sunflower_mag <- c(0.04,0.12,0.12)

erro <- (prod_sunflower_mag - prod_sunflower_pam)/prod_sunflower_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = prod_sunflower_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = prod_sunflower_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Sunflower production (Mt).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "Mt")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, Mt, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```

Regarding yield, all values estimated by MAgPIE exceeded the official data for sunflower.

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
yield_sunflower_pam <- c(2.77,3.38,3.64)
yield_sunflower_mag <- c(1.17,11.0,1.33)

erro <- (yield_sunflower_mag - yield_sunflower_pam)/yield_sunflower_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = yield_sunflower_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = yield_sunflower_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Sunflower yields (t/ha).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "t_per_ha")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, t_per_ha, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```


### Cassava

The total harvested area of cassava for the years 2010, 2015, and 2020 is presented below.   

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

ha_cassav_pam <- c(1.79,1.51,1.21)
ha_cassav_mag <- c(4.89,4.71,4.58)

erro <- (ha_cassav_mag - ha_cassav_pam)/ha_cassav_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = ha_cassav_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = ha_cassav_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Cassava harvested area (Mha).")
```

The PAM/IBGE data indicate that cassava has relatively stable harvested areas between 2010 and 2020, but MAgPIE superestimates its cultivation throughout the decade. The discrepancies are evident in the Northeast and central regions, where the official data show a strong concentration of cassava production that is not captured by the model’s allocation.

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "Mha")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, Mha, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```


```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=15, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

plots <- list() 
for (y in years){
  cropBRA<-crop['BRA',y,'cassav_sp']
  df<-as.data.frame(cropBRA,rev=2)
  names(df)<-c("lon","lat","iso","year","crop","w","value")
  df <- df %>%
    group_by(lon, lat) %>%
    summarise(value = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  df$area_cell<-calArea(df$lon,df$lat,res=0.5,mha=1)
  df$newvalues<-df$area_cell*df$value*100 #ha
  Total_ha=sum(df$newvalues, na.rm = TRUE ) #unit: ha
  Total_Mha=Total_ha/1e6 #unit: Mha

  BRA<-subset(map, country=='BRA')
  coords <- strsplit(BRA$cell, "\\.")  
  mat    <- do.call(rbind, coords) 
  BRA$lon <- mat[,1]
  BRA$lat <- mat[,2]
  BRA$iso <- mat[,3]
  BRA$lon <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lon))
  BRA$lat <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lat))
  
  dfmap <- BRA %>%
    left_join(df, by = c("lon", "lat"))
  
  r <- rasterFromXYZ(cbind(df[,c("lon","lat",
                                   "value","newvalues")], z=1), 
                     res = c(min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lon)))),
                             min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lat))))))
  
  polys <- rasterToPolygons(r, dissolve = FALSE)
  
  crs(polys) <- CRS("+init=EPSG:4326")
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_as_sf(polys) %>%
      st_transform(st_crs(biomes))
  
  
  cells_sf  <- st_make_valid(cells_sf)
    biomes <- st_make_valid(biomes)
  
  
  int_sf <- st_intersection(
      cells_sf  %>% mutate(cell_id = row_number()),
      biomes %>% dplyr::select(CD_LEGEN1))
  
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      filter(!st_is_empty(.)) %>%
      st_collection_extract("POLYGON")
  
  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
      mutate(area = st_area(geometry))
  
  majoritario <- int_sf %>%
      group_by(cell_id) %>%
      slice_max(area, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      dplyr::select(cell_id, CD_LEGEN1)
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_sf %>%
      mutate(cell_id = row_number()) %>%
      left_join(
        st_set_geometry(majoritario, NULL),
        by = "cell_id"
      ) %>%
      dplyr::select(-cell_id)
  
  cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1 <- 
      iconv(cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1, from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8")
  
  cells_bioma <- cells_bioma %>%
      mutate(CD_LEGEN1 = factor(CD_LEGEN1,
                              levels = sort(unique(CD_LEGEN1))))
  
  #vmax <- max(cells_bioma$value, na.rm = TRUE)
  vmax<-0.015
  mean_area<-0.5*vmax
  myplot<-ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data=cells_bioma, aes(fill = value), color=NA) +
    geom_sf(data = biomes, fill = NA, color = "gray", size = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low     = "white",        
      mid     = "orange",       
      high    = "darkred",        
      midpoint= mean_area,
      na.value= "grey",
      name     = "", 
      limits    = c(0, vmax),
      oob    = squish ,
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Cassava",
      subtitle = y,
      caption  = "Source: MAgPIE Default",
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "white")
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank()
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_colorbar(
      barwidth      = unit(0.6, "cm"),
      barheight     = unit(2.5,   "cm"),
    )) +
    theme(
      plot.title        = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = 11),
      plot.caption      = element_text(size = 11),
      legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"),
      legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(size = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor  = element_line(size = 0.25)
    )
    plots<-append(plots, list(myplot))
}
wrap_plots(plots, nrow=1)
```

As in the harvested area, the model overestimated cassava production, with values exceeding the PAM/IBGE data and errors reaching $318\%$.

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
prod_cassav_pam <- c(24.52,23.06,18.20)
prod_cassav_mag <- c(65.32,71.20,76.25)

erro <- (prod_cassav_mag - prod_cassav_pam)/prod_cassav_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = prod_cassav_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = prod_cassav_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Cassava production (Mt).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "Mt")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, Mt, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```

Due to the high production values and the overestimation of the harvested area, the yields estimated by MAgPIE were close to the observed values, with errors ranging from $-4.23\%$ to $+11.07\%$.

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
yield_cassav_pam <- c(13.95,15.24,14.99)
yield_cassav_mag <- c(13.36,15.12,16.65)

erro <- (yield_cassav_mag - yield_cassav_pam)/yield_cassav_pam

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PAM/IBGE" = yield_cassav_pam,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = yield_cassav_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Cassava yields (t/ha).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "t_per_ha")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, t_per_ha, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PAM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```

## Livestock

Raw MAgPIE outputs for the cattle stock proxy were substantially higher than observed PPM/IBGE values, requiring correction for further analysis. First, an analysis of the inputs and outputs of the livestock module was carried out in order to identify which parameters and calculations may be contributing to the large discrepancy observed between MAgPIE results and real-world cattle stock data. It is known that the MAgPIE livestock module is influenced by several key input variables, including: $im\underline{ }pop$: population (million people per year), $pm\underline{ }kcal\underline{ }pc\underline{ }initial$: initial per capita demand (kcal/person-day), $vm\underline{ }cost\underline{ }prod$: production cost factor (in million USD 17MER per year), and $vm\underline{ }prod\underline{ }reg$: regional aggregate production (in million tons of dry matter per year). 

Since the MAgPIE livestock module operates at the regional level—i.e., for Latin America as a whole, which includes Brazil—and no standardized official dataset exists for these parameters at that scale, we estimated the real-world values based on publicly available data sources.
Table below presents a comparison between the default values assumed by MAgPIE and the estimated real-world parameters, using 2010 as the reference year.

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:40%;">

``` 

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

txtliv<- "
'Variable' 'MAgPIE' 'Estimated'
'im_pop'  '590' '597' 
'pm_kcal_pc_initial' '174' '120'
'vm_cost_prod' '128800' '156000'
'vm_prod_reg' '9.13' '620'
"

dfliv <- read.table(text = txtliv, header = TRUE)

reactable::reactable(
  dfliv[, c('Variable', 'MAgPIE', 'Estimated')],  
  highlight = TRUE,
  striped = TRUE,
  bordered = TRUE,
  defaultExpanded = FALSE
)
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:60%;">
```



The variable $vm\underline{ }prod\underline{ }reg$ is the parameter that most significantly diverges from the estimated real-world data. This discrepancy suggests that $vm\underline{ }prod\underline{ }reg$ is a key candidate contributing to the difference observed in cattle numbers, particularly as reflected in the output object $p70\underline{ }cattle\underline{ }stock\underline{ }proxy$, which is the object that contains cattle numbers to fulfill food demand. The calculation of $p70\underline{ }cattle\underline{ }stock\underline{ }proxy$ is based on the following equation:

$$
  \text{p70_cattle_stock_proxy(t,i)} = \frac{\text{im_pop(t,i)}*\text{pm_kcal_pc_initial(t,i,livst_rum)}}{\text{i70_livestock_productivity(t,i,sys_beef)}}
$$

```{=html}
</div>

</div>

``` 

While the variables $im\underline{ }pop$ and $pm\underline{ }kcal\underline{ }pc\underline{ }initial$ appear to be reasonably aligned with real-world data, the variable $i70\underline{ }livestock\underline{ }productivity$ — which represents the productivity indicator for livestock production, expressed in tons of fresh matter per animal per year — will be further investigated to assess whether its values are contributing to the discrepancies observed in cattle numbers. A preliminary analysis indicates that, while MAgPIE assumes a livestock productivity factor of approximately 0.04 tFM/animal/year for ruminant meat in the LAM region, real-world estimates suggest that this value should be closer to 0.70 tFM/animal/year. This substantial discrepancy reinforces the need to reassess the productivity assumptions used in the model, as they may significantly influence the accuracy of cattle stock projections.

After correction, the comparison between official statistics and model outputs reveals a consistent pattern of systematic underestimation of livestock numbers by MAgPIE across all years analyzed. This correction was applied by dividing all MAgPIE values by 10,000. In 2010, the model produced a total of 75.91 million animals compared to 209.54 million reported by PPM/IBGE, corresponding to an overall error of -63.77\%. Similar results are found for 2015 and 2020, with MAgPIE underestimating the total herd by -63.31\% and -65.21\%, respectively .

From a temporal perspective, both datasets indicate herd expansion between 2010 and 2020. Despite the magnitude of underestimation in absolute numbers, the model is able to reproduce the broad spatial distribution of livestock across Brazil . The maps demonstrate that MAgPIE correctly allocates higher cattle densities to the main producing regions, mirroring the spatial patterns reported in PPM/IBGE. However, the systematic bias in herd size highlights that the current livestock proxy in MAgPIE—derived from food and feed demand rather than direct herd counts—does not capture the full scale of Brazil’s cattle sector.


```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:40px;">

<div style="width:50%;">

``` 

```{r}

number_cattle_ppm <- c(209.54,215.22,217.84)
number_cattle_mag <- c(75.91, 78.95, 75.81)

erro <- (number_cattle_mag - number_cattle_ppm)/number_cattle_ppm

df <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PPM/IBGE" = number_cattle_ppm,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = number_cattle_mag,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(df) <- c("2010","2015","2020")

knitr::kable(df, caption = "Brazil livestock numbers (mio. animals).")
```

```{=html}
</div>

<div style="width:50%;">
``` 

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Label = rownames(df)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Label,
               names_to = "Years",
               values_to = "mio_animals")

df_plot <- df_long %>%
  filter(Label != "Error %")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(Years, mio_animals, fill = Label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "PPM/IBGE" = "#6495ed",
    "MAgPIE (Default)" = "#66cdaa"
  )) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{=html}
</div>

</div>
```



## Deforestation

To compare accumulated deforestation in Brazilian territory, official data provided by PRODES/INPE were collected, which are already organized by biome. Consequently, to allow comparison with MAgPIE outputs, changes were calculated for three land types: primary forest, secondary forest, and other land, and the resulting values were aggregated by biome. Both the official data and the model outputs are presented in table below.

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

prodes = c(7.25,2.89,8.96,1.34,1.17,0.54,22.15)
primforest = c(3.05,0.003,0.23,0.15,0.03,0,3.46)
secdforest = c(0.48,0.05,1.74,2.54,0.87,0.19,5.87)
other = c(0.07,0.67,0.59,0.33,0.05,0.22,1.94)
magpie = c(3.6,0.72,2.57,3.02,0.95,0.41,11.27)
  
erro = (magpie - prodes)/prodes

dfd <- data.frame(
  rbind(
  "PRODES/INPE" = prodes,
  "primforest" = primforest,
  "secdforest" = secdforest,
  "other" = other,
  "MAgPIE (Default)" = magpie,
  "Error %" = round(erro*100,2)
)
)

colnames(dfd) <- c("Amazon","Caatinga","Cerrado", "Atlantic Forest", "Pampa", "Pantanal", "Total")

knitr::kable(dfd, caption = "Cumulated deforestation 2010 - 2020 (Mha)") %>%
  row_spec(c(2,3,4), background = "#D7DFF7")  %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = TRUE)
```

The largest discrepancy in the deforestation data is observed in the Atlantic Forest biome, where the error reaches 128.79\% compared to official statistics. The spatial distribution simulated by MAgPIE is presented below.

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
d_prim<-land['BRA','y2010','primforest']-land['BRA','y2020','primforest']
d_secd<-land['BRA','y2010','secdforest']-land['BRA','y2020','secdforest']
d_other<-land['BRA','y2010','other']-land['BRA','y2020','other']

des_prim<-as.data.frame(d_prim,rev=2)
des_secd<-as.data.frame(d_secd,rev=2)
des_other<-as.data.frame(d_other,rev=2)

names(des_prim)=c("lon","lat","iso","year1","year2","land","value")
names(des_secd)=c("lon","lat","iso","year1","year2","land","value")
names(des_other)=c("lon","lat","iso","year1","year2","land","value")

des_prim$value <- lapply(des_prim$value, function(x) pmax(x, 0))
des_secd$value <- lapply(des_secd$value, function(x) pmax(x, 0))
des_other$value <- lapply(des_other$value, function(x) pmax(x, 0))

desmatamento_br<-des_prim[c(1:5)]
desmatamento_br$value<-as.numeric(des_prim$value)+as.numeric(des_secd$value)+as.numeric(des_other$value)

desmatamento_br$area_cell<-calArea(desmatamento_br$lon,desmatamento_br$lat,res=0.5,mha=1)
desmatamento_br$newvalues<-desmatamento_br$area_cell*desmatamento_br$value*100 #ha

map<-readRDS("clustermap_rev4.117_c200_67420_h12.rds")
BRA<-subset(map, country=='BRA')
coords <- strsplit(BRA$cell, "\\.")  
mat    <- do.call(rbind, coords) 
BRA$lon <- mat[,1]
BRA$lat <- mat[,2]
BRA$iso <- mat[,3]
BRA$lon <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lon))
BRA$lat <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lat))

df <- BRA %>%
  left_join(desmatamento_br, by = c("lon", "lat"))

r <- rasterFromXYZ(cbind(df[,c("lon","lat", "value", "newvalues")], z=1), 
                   res = c(min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lon)))),
                           min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lat))))))

polys <- rasterToPolygons(r, dissolve = FALSE)

crs(polys) <- CRS("+init=EPSG:4326")

cells_sf  <- st_as_sf(polys) %>%
  st_transform(st_crs(biomes))

cells_sf  <- st_make_valid(cells_sf)
biomes <- st_make_valid(biomes)

int_sf <- st_intersection(
  cells_sf  %>% mutate(cell_id = row_number()),
  biomes %>% dplyr::select(CD_LEGEN1))

int_sf <- int_sf %>%
  filter(!st_is_empty(.)) %>%
  st_collection_extract("POLYGON")

int_sf <- int_sf %>%
  mutate(area = st_area(geometry))

majoritario <- int_sf %>%
  group_by(cell_id) %>%
  slice_max(area, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(cell_id, CD_LEGEN1)

cells_bioma <- cells_sf %>%
  mutate(cell_id = row_number()) %>%
  left_join(
    st_set_geometry(majoritario, NULL),
    by = "cell_id"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-cell_id)

cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1 <- 
  iconv(cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1, from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8")

cells_bioma <- cells_bioma %>%
  mutate(CD_LEGEN1 = factor(CD_LEGEN1,
                            levels = sort(unique(CD_LEGEN1))))

vmax <- 0.05
mean_area<-0.5*vmax
breaks_vec <- seq(0, vmax, by = 0.01)
labels_vec <- comma(breaks_vec)
labels_vec[length(labels_vec)]<-''
myplot<-ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data=cells_bioma, aes(fill = value), color=NA) +
  geom_sf(data = biomes, fill = NA, color = "gray", size = 0.6) +
 
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low     = "white",        
    mid     = "orange",       
    high    = "darkred",        
    midpoint= mean_area,
    na.value= "grey",
    name     = "", 
    limits    = c(0, vmax),
    oob    = squish ,
    breaks    = breaks_vec,            
    labels    = labels_vec,            
    ) +
  labs(
    title = "Deforestation",
    subtitle = "2010-2020",
    caption  = "Source: MAgPIE/Default version",
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "white")
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(
    barwidth      = unit(1, "cm"),
    barheight     = unit(5,   "cm"),
  )) +
  theme(
    plot.title        = element_text(size = 26, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = 24),
    plot.caption      = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text       = element_text(size = 20),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(size = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(size = 0.25)
  )
print(myplot)

```

Now, the contribution of each land class is shown, providing relevant information on the dynamics of deforestation. In the Amazon biome, most deforestation is associated with primary forests, whereas in the Caatinga it is predominantly classified as other land. As previously noted, the largest changes were related to secondary forests, with particularly significant values observed in the Atlantic Forest and Cerrado biomes.

```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=15, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
plots<-list()
typeland<-c("primforest", "secdforest", "other")
for (l in typeland){
  d<-land['BRA','y2010',l]-land['BRA','y2020',l]
  des<-as.data.frame(d,rev=2)
  names(des)=c("lon","lat","iso","year1","year2","land","value")
  des$value <- lapply(des$value, function(x) pmax(x, 0))
  desmat_br<-des[c(1:5)]
  desmat_br$value<-as.numeric(des$value)
  
  desmat_br$area_cell<-calArea(desmat_br$lon,desmat_br$lat,res=0.5,mha=1)
  desmat_br$newvalues<-desmat_br$area_cell*desmat_br$value*100 #ha
  
  map<-readRDS("clustermap_rev4.117_c200_67420_h12.rds")
  BRA<-subset(map, country=='BRA')
  coords <- strsplit(BRA$cell, "\\.")  
  mat    <- do.call(rbind, coords) 
  BRA$lon <- mat[,1]
  BRA$lat <- mat[,2]
  BRA$iso <- mat[,3]
  BRA$lon <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lon))
  BRA$lat <- as.numeric(gsub("p", ".", BRA$lat))

  df <- BRA %>%
    left_join(desmat_br, by = c("lon", "lat"))

  r <- rasterFromXYZ(cbind(df[,c("lon","lat", "value", "newvalues")], z=1), 
                   res = c(min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lon)))),
                           min(diff(sort(unique(BRA$lat))))))

  polys <- rasterToPolygons(r, dissolve = FALSE)

  crs(polys) <- CRS("+init=EPSG:4326")

  cells_sf  <- st_as_sf(polys) %>%
    st_transform(st_crs(biomes))

  cells_sf  <- st_make_valid(cells_sf)
  biomes <- st_make_valid(biomes)

  int_sf <- st_intersection(
    cells_sf  %>% mutate(cell_id = row_number()),
    biomes %>% dplyr::select(CD_LEGEN1))

  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
    filter(!st_is_empty(.)) %>%
    st_collection_extract("POLYGON")

  int_sf <- int_sf %>%
    mutate(area = st_area(geometry))

  majoritario <- int_sf %>%
    group_by(cell_id) %>%
    slice_max(area, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(cell_id, CD_LEGEN1)

  cells_bioma <- cells_sf %>%
    mutate(cell_id = row_number()) %>%
    left_join(
      st_set_geometry(majoritario, NULL),
    by = "cell_id"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(-cell_id)

  cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1 <- 
    iconv(cells_bioma$CD_LEGEN1, from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8")

  cells_bioma <- cells_bioma %>%
    mutate(CD_LEGEN1 = factor(CD_LEGEN1,
                            levels = sort(unique(CD_LEGEN1))))

  vmax <- 0.05
  mean_area<-0.5*vmax
  breaks_vec <- seq(0, vmax, by = 0.01)
  labels_vec <- comma(breaks_vec)
  labels_vec[length(labels_vec)]<-''
  myplot<-ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data=cells_bioma, aes(fill = value), color=NA) +
    geom_sf(data = biomes, fill = NA, color = "gray", size = 0.6) +
 
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low     = "white",        
      mid     = "orange",       
      high    = "darkred",        
      midpoint= mean_area,
      na.value= "grey",
      name     = "", 
      limits    = c(0, vmax),
      oob    = squish ,
      breaks    = breaks_vec,            
      labels    = labels_vec,            
      ) +
    labs(
      title = l,
      subtitle = "Deforestation 2010-2020",
      caption  = "Source: MAgPIE/Default version",
    ) +
  
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "white")
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank()
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_colorbar(
      barwidth      = unit(1, "cm"),
      barheight     = unit(5,   "cm"),
    )) +
    theme(
      plot.title        = element_text(size = 26, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = 24),
      plot.caption      = element_text(size = 22),
      legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"),
      legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 20),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(size = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor  = element_line(size = 0.25)
    )
  plots<-append(plots,myplot)
}

wrap_plots(plots, nrow=1)


```


Comparisons conducted for deforestation highlight the need for model adjustments to more accurately reflect the Brazilian context, including considerations of the Forest Code and the protection of designated conservation areas.


## Synthesis of the main results

The results highlight a substantial need for further refinement of MAgPIE in order to more accurately reflect the Brazilian reality. Overall, the following key points emerge from the topics evaluated:

### Crops

The results for the agricultural sector indicate that soybean was associated with comparatively smaller errors than the other crops across the evaluated years. In contrast, maize, sugarcane, and cotton were consistently underestimated by MAgPIE, with simulated values lower than official statistics in all three years. Regarding yields, MAgPIE tended to overestimate sugarcane productivity, while the opposite pattern was observed for maize and cotton, for which the model underestimated yields.

With respect to harvested area, the results follow a pattern similar to the previous analyses: sugarcane and cotton were consistently underestimated, whereas soybean and maize showed comparatively smaller deviations. Spatial distribution, however, reveals notable limitations of the model in representing the Brazilian context. For soybean, the largest discrepancy occurred in the Caatinga biome, where MAgPIE projects substantial cultivation absent from official records. Divergences were also evident in other regions: for maize, the greatest inconsistency appeared in the Pampa biome, while for cotton, the most pronounced differences were found in the Atlantic Forest, Pampa, and Cerrado. These findings underscore the need for further refinement of the model’s spatial allocation mechanisms to more accurately reflect national production patterns.

### Livestock

The comparison between MAgPIE raw outputs and official PPM/IBGE data reveals systematic discrepancies in livestock numbers. Across 2010, 2015, and 2020, MAgPIE consistently overestimates the total cattle herd by more than three orders of magnitude. Despite this strong bias in absolute values, the model captures the broad spatial distribution of herds across Brazil, reproducing the relative concentration of livestock in the Cerrado, Atlantic Forest, and Amazon biomes. From a temporal perspective, both datasets indicate an expansion of the Brazilian cattle herd between 2010 and 2020, and the model broadly mirrors this trend in spatial allocation.

Input–output relations of the livestock module were analyzed to identify potential sources of discrepancy. Among the key inputs considered, regional aggregate production diverged most sharply from real-world estimates. Preliminary checks showed that population and demand values align reasonably with observations, whereas productivity assumptions are problematic.

After correction by a factor of 10,000, MAgPIE results consistently underestimated Brazilian livestock numbers by about -63\% to -65\% compared to PPM/IBGE. At the biome scale, errors were largest in the Cerrado and Atlantic Forest (-67\% to -70\%), while the Caatinga showed smaller deviations (-27\% to -34\%). This suggests that post-processing corrections or recalibration of the livestock proxy will be necessary for aligning MAgPIE results with observed statistics.

### Deforestation

In the case of deforestation, the results indicate the need for model adjustments tailored to the Brazilian context, as protected areas were identified with losses of primary forest. Similarly, the behavior of secondary forests also revealed inconsistencies that require further analysis and adaptation in order to more accurately represent land-use dynamics in Brazil.

# Download Full Report (pdf)

The detailed results and discussions are presented in a comprehensive report available in PDF format. For a complete analysis, please download the full report using the button below.

```{=html}
<a href="MAgPIE_PreliminaryValidation_Report_v2 1.pdf" download class="btn btn-primary" style="padding:10px 16px; font-size:16px; border-radius:6px; text-decoration:none;">
  Download Full Report (PDF)
</a>
```

# References
